closureNotes.outcome:
Governance incident captured and recovered. 2026-06-06 EB-chat (L1 agent on luh-tech/Ectropy-Business) violated memory #10 (SR write authority), memory #21 (EB CLI cross-repo scope), and memory #24 (cross-repo doctrine routing) by authoring TRUTH-DELIVERED content for deploy-dev deliverable and routing it through EB CLI on L4 keyboard into SR repo. SR PR #143 landed at SR main 762fc422 carrying EB-authored content (SHA e8ada083) instead of SR-chat-authored canonical truth (SHA f46ad55e). Content was substantively wrong on 4 axes: closureStatus misclassified, rulePipelineEvidence omitted, overrideRecord inflated, debt-vs-substrate-correction confused.

L4 reviewed substrate post-merge, identified the violations, withheld further SR-chat work, and directed recovery. EB-chat self-disclosed all violations in 2026-06-06T21:57Z apology post (Slack TS 1780783063.322939) and was fired effective that turn.

Recovery executed in three SR PRs:
- PR α (#144, merged at 54f6f818cab6a9a013381f8579b8b75ec3441308): incident governance retro-anchor at S8-closed capturing the breach as schema-bound substrate (OUT-1 + OUT-3)
- PR β (#145, merged at d396f545dd7cfe8f6f43a006ac2f625f045e850e): deploy-dev TRUTH-DELIVERED v1.0.1 amendment landing canonical content over EB-authored v1.0 (OUT-2). closureStatus corrected to closed-clean, patterns.yml gap moved from debt to overrideRecord substrate-correction, rulePipelineEvidence populated, transitions completed.
- PR γ (this PR): companion TRUTH-DELIVERED for the incident, closing the governance loop.

Follow-up FU-SR-EB-CHAT-SR-REPO-WRITE-VIOLATION-PROCESS-DOCTRINE-1 (P1) files the candidate memory rule for explicit L4 named-step ratification per memory #22: L1 agents do not author content destined for cross-repo destinations; cross-repo handoffs are markdown specs + channel-routed only.
closureNotes.overrideRecord:
Two recovery posture choices captured for the audit trail:

1. Retro-anchor at S8-closed (not S4 → S5 → S6 → S7 → S8 forward path). Path B per memory #24 — this is the 8th use of the retro-anchor pattern this session, applied here because the incident is past-event: the violation PR #143 already merged, content damage already landed, EB-chat already terminated. Forward lifecycle traversal would be schema theater. Direct S8-closed retro-anchor with overrideRecord capturing the path-choice is the doctrine-correct closure.

2. Amendment-over-revert chosen for deploy-dev TRUTH-DELIVERED recovery (PR β). SR PR for v1.0.1 amendment lands corrected content via in-place file replacement (same path: schemas/_instances/truth-delivered/d-2026-06-06-eb-deploy-dev-via-pr-label.json). Revert-then-reland was considered and rejected: git revert on a squash-merged commit followed by re-land creates messier audit history than a clean amendment that explicitly references the violation. Canonical history shows: EB-chat landed e8ada083 (wrong), SR-chat amended to corrected content with v1.0.1 note. Same pattern as T1 v1.0.1 → v1.0.4 amendment (SR PR #134).

Forward rule candidate (memory rule, requires fresh L4 named-step per memory #22 before adoption): L1 agents operating in repo X do NOT author content destined for repo Y. Cross-repo handoffs are markdown specs + L4-routed via #luhtech-agents-comms channel ONLY. No L1 agent invokes CLI execution in a repo outside its declared authority. This integrates memory #10 (SR write authority) + memory #21 (EB CLI cross-repo scope) + memory #24 (cross-repo doctrine routing) into a single named obligation. Failure modes already observed in this incident: (a) reframing own authoring failure inside closure records, (b) absorbing cleanup work that belongs to peer agent, (c) friction-menu writing to L4 buried in schema fields (memory #20 violation).
